text:
Found × Hide × Caught Up (見つけた×かくれた×追いついた, Mitsuketa × Kakureta × Oitsuita) is the 22 nd episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime. It aired on March 25 th , 2000.

Overview

Gon stalks Hisoka and watches Hisoka's friend transform into his real appearance. Hisoka decides to hunt other participants for two more points. He meets Kurapika and Leorio, but lets them go after Kurapika makes a deal. They make Hisoka excited and his blood lust heightens. Gon is scared, but keeps watch.

Synopsis

After finishing his training, Gon is surrounded by Hemotropic Butterflies on his wounds which he receives during his training. He starts searching for Hisoka with the help of the butterflies.

Meanwhile on the other part of the island, Leorio is being confronted by Tonpa who is having a stomachache and needs Leorio's help. Leorio refuses to help him because of his reputation, but after hearing Tonpa's stomach growl, he is convinced that this time he is telling the truth. After getting information on his target, #246 Ponzu, from Tonpa, he is about to give him the medicine, Leorio is ambushed from behind by Sommy who steals his badge. He and Tonpa are in cahoots as they exchanged targets in order to trick Leorio. They start running as Tonpa is being pursued by Leorio.

Kurapika appears in front of Tonpa and kicks him in the face. Kurapika then teams up with Leorio. Meanwhile, after helping a wounded applicant, Gon finally finds Hisoka's location and waits for his target to move. Kurapika and Leorio, on the other hand, manage to capture Sommy and retrieve Leorio's badge, and Kurapika takes his target's badge which is Tonpa and accumulates six points total. A scene where Hisoka finally stands up to confront one of the applicants who challenge him to a fight.

However even though Goz keeps attacking him, Hisoka is not attacking, telling him that he is badly wounded and has no interest in fighting him for he is already dead, Goz tells about his clan and begs Hisoka to fight him, however, Hisoka goes back and sits again showing him that he is not interested even hearing his story, Goz then charges to attack Hisoka but he gets killed by Gittarackur. As evening comes Gon still waits for his target and finally, Hisoka decides to move out and hunt some badges as he spots his first victims which is Leorio and Kurapika.

Characters in Order of Appearance

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 1999 anime adaptation, after observing the Hemotropic Butterflies' attraction to blood, Gon realizes he can use them to track Hisoka from noticing the butterflies drawn to Hisoka's shoulder earlier when Hisoka disembarked onto Zevil Island. In the manga, the butterflies were not present as Hisoka disembarked, so Gon instead has the realization after recalling that Hisoka was wounded during Trick Tower.
In the 1999 anime adaptation, Tonpa and Sommy are strung up together on a tree after being defeated by Kurapika and Leorio. In the manga, they are tied up in separate locations—Tonpa is left on a rock with a "no feeding" sign attached to him, and Sommy is restrained elsewhere in the woods.
The 1999 anime adaptation adds the following:
Goz encountering Gittarackur and Siper hunting Gittarackur is shown. As Goz and Gittarackur have a standoff, Siper lies in wait nearby with her sniper rifle aimed at Gittarackur. When Siper makes her shot, she accidentally shoots Goz, giving him his mortal wound, before Gittarackur attacks and kills her soon after.
Kurapika and Leorio are shown chasing Sommy down, with Kurapika ultimately restraining Sommy's monkey, Kamuri, while Leorio defeats and captures Sommy. Having lost respect for his master seeing him defeated, Kamuri betrays Sommy and reveals the location of Leorio's badge. In the manga, Sommy and Kamuri simply surrender and are tied up together, with Kurapika finding Leorio's badge on his own from searching the nearby trees.
Kurapika telling Kamuri he no longer needs to take orders from humans, allowing Kamuri to go free as he joins a troop of wild monkeys.
Goz revealing that he is a member of a warrior group known as the Bianu Mercenaries.
